biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::EGFR Classic (ex19del,L858R)
biomarkers.name: EGFR Classic (ex19del,L858R)
biomarkers.notes: Complete genotyping via tissue or plasma NGS. At resistance/progression, test via plasma or tissue rebiopsy for secondary resistance mechanisms (e.g., MET amplification, EGFR T790M, HER2 amplification, new gene fusions). For Lazertinib+Amivantamab, administer prophylactic anticoagulation, scalp clindamycin, and dexamethasone premedication.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::EGFR Atypical (S768I, L861Q, G719X)
biomarkers.name: EGFR Atypical (S768I, L861Q, G719X)
biomarkers.notes: Identified via broad multiplex somatic NGS panels. Ensure individual variants are checked against OncoKB or FDA labels to verify TKI sensitivity status.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::EGFR Exon 20 Insertions
biomarkers.name: EGFR Exon 20 Insertions
biomarkers.notes: Missed by standard allele-specific PCR in up to 50% of cases; requires broad DNA- or RNA-based NGS for precise insertions mapping. Subcutaneous amivantamab and hyalilionidase-lpuj may substitute for the IV formulation.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::ALK Fusions
biomarkers.name: ALK Fusions
biomarkers.notes: Detected via break-apart FISH or broad panel NGS. RNA-based NGS is preferred to maximize fusion transcript detection when DNA testing is negative. Immunotherapy is associated with zero to minimal clinical benefit in this cohort.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::ROS1 Fusions
biomarkers.name: ROS1 Fusions
biomarkers.notes: FISH testing identifies chromosomal rearrangement to infer a fusion, but confirmation or primary discovery via NGS is standard. Brain-penetrant agents (Repotrectinib, Taletrectinib, Entrectinib) are strongly preferred for central nervous system disease.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::BRAF V600E
biomarkers.name: BRAF V600E
biomarkers.notes: DNA NGS or targeted real-time PCR assays. Explicitly separate algorithms based on whether the mutation is discovered prior to or during first-line chemotherapy. Distinct from non-V600E BRAF mutations which lack targeted TKI approvals.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::KRAS G12C
biomarkers.name: KRAS G12C
biomarkers.notes: Readily identified via standard somatic DNA NGS. Highly prevalent in squamous and non-squamous tumors with tobacco history. TKIs are typically deployed in the subsequent-line setting after chemo-immunology combinations.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::NTRK 1/2/3 Fusions
biomarkers.name: NTRK 1/2/3 Fusions
biomarkers.notes: Fusions are structurally complex; DNA-based NGS panels frequently miss NTRK rearrangements due to large intronic regions. RNA-based NGS is highly recommended if DNA testing yields a wild-type driver profile.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::MET Exon 14 Skipping
biomarkers.name: MET Exon 14 Skipping
biomarkers.notes: Requires NGS coverage that spans the splice donor and acceptor sites of MET Exon 14. Distinct from MET amplification (FISH driven) and HGFR/c-Met protein overexpression (IHC driven via clone SP44 for antibody-drug conjugates).

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::RET Fusions
biomarkers.name: RET Fusions
biomarkers.notes: Identified via multi-gene NGS panels. RNA-based NGS is complementary and preferred over standard DNA testing if no other driver oncogene is discovered. High risk of intracranial metastases; select highly brain-penetrant RET inhibitors.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::ERBB2 (HER2) Mutations
biomarkers.name: ERBB2 (HER2) Mutations
biomarkers.notes: Activating mutations primarily occur as insertions in the tyrosine kinase domain (Exon 20). Zongertinib and sevabertinib are FDA-approved for tyrosine kinase domain mutations. There is an absence of prospective data reporting cross-efficacy of zongertinib after sevabertinib or vice versa. HER2 amplification/overexpression is distinct and does not mandate these TKIs.

biomarkers.id: Track C - Biomarker Driver Positive::NRG1 Gene Fusions
biomarkers.name: NRG1 Gene Fusions
biomarkers.notes: NRG1 gene fusions lead to inappropriate signaling via the ERBB family. RNA-based NGS is strictly preferable to DNA-based NGS because large introns in NRG1 are commonly excluded from DNA panels. IHC is not a suitable detection method; FISH requires NGS confirmation

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IA1, IA2, IA3 | Biomarker negetive (Wild type)
biomarkers.name: Stage IA1, IA2, IA3 | Biomarker negetive (Wild type)
biomarkers.notes: Radiation therapy (SABR) is strictly preferred only if the patient is medically inoperable due to lung/heart function.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IA1, IA2, IA3 | Biomarker Positive (EGFR / ALK / Others)
biomarkers.name: Stage IA1, IA2, IA3 | Biomarker Positive (EGFR / ALK / Others)
biomarkers.notes: For medically inoperable patients, proceed to localized SABR/RT. Do not utilize systemic TKIs or IO here.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.name: Stage IB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.notes: No Immunotherapy Approved. High-risk includes poorly differentiated tumors, vascular invasion, wedge resection, or tumors $\ge 4$ cm.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.name: Stage IB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.notes: Driven by the ADAURA trial (NCCN/ASCO Category 1). Immunotherapy is completely omitted due to low driver efficacy.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IIA / IIB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIA / IIB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.notes: Upfront surgery requires post-op chemo followed by Pembrolizumab for Stage IIB if PD-L1 >= 1%. Pemetrexed is completely restricted to non-squamous tumors.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IIA / IIB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIA / IIB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.notes: Bypassing pre-op immunotherapy avoids long-half-life IO cross-over toxicity, reducing subsequent severe TKI-induced pneumonitis

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IIA / IIB | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.name: Stage IIA / IIB | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.notes: Driven by the ALINA trial (ASCO/NCCN approved). High brain penetration provides robust central nervous system protection.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::Stage IIA / IIB | Other Drivers (ROS1, RET, BRAF, KRAS, MET, etc.)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIA / IIB | Other Drivers (ROS1, RET, BRAF, KRAS, MET, etc.)
biomarkers.notes: Targeted TKIs for these rarer drivers lack routine adjuvant approval in early stages. Immunotherapy is withheld due to poor driver benefit.

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::None | Driver negative
biomarkers.name: None | Driver negative

biomarkers.id: Track A - Localized / Curative Intent::None | Driver positive
biomarkers.name: None | Driver positive

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.notes: Neoadjuvant chemo-IO significantly enhances pathologic complete response (pCR) in wild-type tumors

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.notes: Under the ADAURA protocol, Stage IIIA drives the highest absolute overall survival benefit from adjuvant Osimertinib.

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.notes: Under the ALINA protocol, patients bypass post-op chemo toxicity and transition straight to oral target-directed therapy.

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | Biomarker Negative (Wild-Type)
biomarkers.notes: Mandated for patients showing no disease progression during chemoradiation, provided PD-L1 >= 1%.

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | EGFR Classic Positive (ex19del / L858R)
biomarkers.notes: The PACIFIC Override: These patients bypass consolidation Durvalumab. Osimertinib replaces it to prevent systemic and brain relapse.

biomarkers.id: Track B - Locally Advanced / Regional::Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.name: Stage IIIA / IIIB / IIIC | ALK Fusion Positive
biomarkers.notes: Consolidation Durvalumab is routinely withheld due to zero benefit and high pneumonitis risk post-radiation.

drugs.id: the sclc hybrid loophole: nccn explicitly recognizes lcnec as a highly aggressive variant. early stages (i
drugs.display_name: The SCLC Hybrid Loophole: NCCN explicitly recognizes LCNEC as a highly aggressive variant. Early Stages (I–III): Surgical resection followed by Small Cell Lung Cancer (SCLC)-style adjuvant chemotherapy (Etoposide + Cisplatin/Carboplatin). Advanced Setting (Stage IV): Can be treated with non-squamous regimens OR SCLC-style Platinum/Etoposide +/- Immunotherapy.
